description.txt: A workspace Owner using Soleur as their AI organization. Workspaces may have MULTIPLE Owners (ADR-038, ADR-073 team workspaces); Owner-shared surfaces (e.g. the operator email-triage inbox, ADR-066) are readable by every Owner, not just one founder.
Vendors, regulators, and counsel emailing the operator address. Their statutory/operational mail is the email-triage inbox's inbound source.

A beta tester or prospect the operator is in sales/product conversations with. The origin of the third-party contact + conversation PII captured in the owner-private beta-CRM (beta_contacts / interview_notes). An involuntary data subject (Art. 14); no direct access to the store.

An untrusted contributor opening a pull request. Their PR-head code is executed only by the fix-constraints Stage A producer (pull_request, contents:read, no write token) — never by the privileged Stage B consumer, which opens a data-only draft follow-up PR from the default branch (ADR-074).
Claude LLM for agent reasoning and tool use
Source control, CI/CD, issue tracking, and releases
DNS, CDN, R2 storage, and zero-trust tunnel
Centralized secrets management with runtime injection
Community notifications and release announcements
Payment processing and subscription billing
Privacy-focused website analytics
Inbound email ingress (operator-inbox webhook, svix-verified) + transactional outbound (statutory triage notifications). ADR-066.

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest/git-data heartbeats, and a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK self-reports from the deny-all registry host and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is issue-alerts.tf/cron-monitors.tf/uptime-monitors.tf, so versions/main/variables changes do NOT auto-apply). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

Keyless container-image signing/verification (cosign/Fulcio/Rekor). The release CI keyless-signs the pushed web app image digest (this IS a live sigstore call — github → sigstore below). The host then cosign-verifies the signature FULLY OFFLINE against a pinned trusted_root.json — it makes NO live sigstore call at verify time (ADR-087, #6005) — supply-chain authenticity for the shared host (#5933 Item 4).

The connected repo's own committed plugins/soleur/ dir, present in the user's workspace when they connect a Soleur-dogfooding repo. UNTRUSTED: its hooks/hooks.json command-hooks would run in the Node dispatch process (outside bwrap). INERT for the SDK — both real-SDK factories load plugins:[{path: getPluginPath()}] (the platform-deployed root), never this copy (ADR-093 trust boundary).

Full autonomous pipeline: plan, work, review, compound, ship
ADR lifecycle and C4 diagram generation
Engineering assessment, architecture decision detection
Marketing assessment, content opportunities

Severity-ranked operational-notification store (ADR-085, #6007). Mutable read/act/archive state, workspace-grain, Owner-shared reads — SEPARATE from the email-triage WORM statutory ledger. Content-minimized (title + source_ref ids only). GET /api/inbox merges it with email-triage into one severity-ordered feed (statutory pinned first).

Owner-private beta-tester / prospect CRM (ADR-102, #6165). Third-party contact PII + dual-lens conversation notes + append-only stage-transition history. Owner-only RLS; writes RPC-only via auth.uid()-pinned SECURITY DEFINER functions; the cro/cpo agents read AND write via the crm_* MCP tools (agent-native parity). 24-month retention; Art. 17 via ON DELETE CASCADE + crm_erase_contact.

platform.infra.tunnel.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel, N connector replicas — 2 today: web-1 + web-2)

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.) and MULTIPLE connector replicas: every web host runs cloudflared against it, so CF load-balances across them and `localhost:` means 'whichever replica answered', not 'this host'. Correctness therefore requires every connector host to be a 10.0.1.0/24 member — see ADR-114 (I1/I2) for the invariants and #6416 for the failure. Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

platform.infra.tunnel.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers; per-user worktrees on host-local NVMe (ADR-068). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921)

Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders. Extracted to its own single-host private-net node so exactly-one-scheduler is enforced by topology, unblocking active-active web (ADR-030, ADR-100, #6178)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.technology: PostgreSQL — dedicated EU project, Supavisor session pooler :5432

description.txt: Inngest config + run history. Dedicated project isolates connection budget + fate from the main app DB (#5450). Public tables RLS-locked; reachable only as the postgres owner (ADR-030 I8)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.title: Supabase PostgreSQL (Inngest)

description.txt: Inngest durable queue + run-state. AOF on the persistent volume survives a host re-provision — the armed-reminder durability fix (#5450)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)
Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.technology: Bare git repos over private net

description.txt: Shared bare git repos (objects/refs) over the private net; per-user worktrees stay host-local on NVMe. The git-data host holds the per-ref monotonic max generation and writer-side-CAS-rejects stale lease writes (ADR-068)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.title: Shared git-data

description.txt: Self-hosted EU Redis — ADR-059 replay buffer + session/control state; network-reachable, per-workspace key namespacing, TLS + requirepass. DISTINCT node from the loopback Inngest Redis (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

ts0mr7.title: registry. ingress → tcp://10.0.1.30:5000. ts0mr7.technology: cloudflared access tcp → raw TCP over WebSocket (tcp://, NOT http:// — #6122)

1hsontv.title: CI reaches the deny-all-public zot host to dual-push release images, and reaches web-1's shell for the 12 terraform_data SSH provisioners — both ONLY through this tunnel, via CF Access service tokens (separate tokens per route, so registry-write rotates independently of host-shell access)
1hsontv.technology: cloudflared access tcp/ssh (CF Access service token)

1xh9vn6.title: Every web host runs a cloudflared connector against the ONE tunnel (2 replicas today). CF load-balances across them, so ssh:// and deploy. ingress land non-deterministically — the reason the deploy path needs ADR-068's private-net fan-out and the SSH provisioners carry an in-band hostname tripwire (ADR-114 I2).

16ifn2x.title: Workstream tab: reads connected-repo issues (REST) + Project v2 board Status (GraphQL); AND writes issues — create / title / label-driven status / close-reopen — via the audited GitHub App-token client (ADR-109). The board is the canonical Status store (ADR-097); a status write is a label mutation that the board-status-sync workflow (github-internal) mirrors. owner/repo/installation resolve from the active workspace (ADR-044), never request input.

i04e4.title: Statutory triage notifications (single configured recipient; reads are Owner-shared per ADR-066)

1cyl7e.title: Ships journald + host_metrics via Vector (arm64, aarch64 build) to the isolated Logs source 2457081; token in soleur-inngest/prd (ADR-100 Phase-1 caveat resolved, #6197)

37une5.title: Ships journald + host_metrics via Vector to the shared Logs source 2457081 (per-host host_name discriminator; token BETTERSTACK_LOGS_TOKEN in soleur/prd); ungated from web_colocate_inngest so every web host ships logs post-ADR-100 (#6396)

a4suot.title: Hosts (dedicated single-host node, private-net 10.0.1.40; removed from web cloud-init — ADR-100, #6178)

8r6n1l.title: Pulls app image + baked bootstrap scripts/hooks + the cosign .sig at boot/deploy — zot-primary, dark-launch gated (attempts zot only when configured + /v2/-reachable + login ok); the SAME @sha256 digest dual-pushed from CI; verify is offline against the unchanged pinned root, no live sigstore call (ADR-096, #6122)

1cnia11.title: Atomic fallback pull when zot is unconfigured/unreachable — dual-pushed + break-glass through the Phase-5 soak, then retired; authenticated (read:packages), immutable @sha256 digest, offline cosign verify (#5921, #5887, #6005)

16wb678.title: Origin uptime probe at the LB-fronted app.soleur.ai surface (betteruptime_monitor.app/soleur_apex) — covers web-1 (the app A-record → web-1, dns.tf); the #5933 per-host web-<n> probe was RETIRED (CF 521, no per-host origin vhost). web-2 warm standby has NO standing uptime coverage — its dead-boot page is the #6396 Sentry terminal-stage issue-alert

1g31eeh.title: Keyless-signs the released app image digest (cosign, OIDC→Fulcio→Rekor) (#5933 Item 4)

ygkzni.title: Injects secrets — dedicated host's boot credential is scoped to the SEPARATE Doppler project `soleur-inngest` (its own `prd` root holds only inngest secrets: Postgres URI, freshly-rotated signing/event keys, Redis pw), NOT a `prd` branch config that would inherit all ~116 soleur/prd secrets (ADR-100, #6178; mirrors the soleur-registry isolation #6122)

19fos3w.title: Injects the registry-host boot credential (scoped to the isolated soleur-registry project's prd root — the 3 admitted secrets ZOT_PULL/PUSH_TOKEN + BETTERSTACK_LOGS_TOKEN, no soleur/prd inheritance)

2p5fpf.title: Ships disk-state observability — df% of /var/lib/zot + resize2fs before/after + zot health — as one SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK event, plus a SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC private-NIC convergence event (#6415), to the isolated Logs source 2457081; token in soleur-registry/prd, queried via betterstack-query.sh --grep SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK|SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC (#6244/#6415)

64kshd.title: Polls the SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK + SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC Logs sources (ClickHouse SQL via betterstack-query.sh) for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm, the private-NIC convergence alarm (#6415), and follow-through soak probes

n779bv.title: Host-side fatal + deploy telemetry, POSTed straight to the store API with the baked ${sentry_dsn} — the boot fatal-emit trap armed at the TOP of `runcmd:` in cloud-init.yml (before Doppler exists), ci-deploy.sh's deploy/sandbox-canary/cosign-verify/zot-fallback events, and container-restart-monitor.sh. Fail-open and SSH-free by construction: this is how a host that never finished booting still pages

5x2z8j.title: Exceptions + debounced warns via the @sentry/nextjs SDK (sentry.client.config.ts / sentry.server.config.ts / instrumentation.ts, SENTRY_DSN) — the stack-keyed capture shape the issue alerts group on; AND the Inngest-fired crons' end-of-run check-ins (`postSentryHeartbeat` in _cron-shared.ts → /api/{id}/cron/{slug}/{key}/?status=ok|error, bounded retry #5728) that satisfy the 43 Inngest-substrate sentry_cron_monitors
5x2z8j.technology: HTTPS (Sentry SDK envelope + cron check-in API)

hb3093.title: CI is both an emitter and the applier: the ./.github/actions/sentry-heartbeat composite posts ok|error check-ins from 5 workflows — 3 GHA-`schedule:`-fired (scheduled-inngest-health, -zot-restart-loop, -realtime-probe) and 2 `workflow_dispatch`-only that Inngest DISPATCHES per ADR-033 (scheduled-terraform-drift, -domain-model-drift). Those 2 are the third substrate: Inngest-scheduled but GHA-executed, so their check-in rides this edge, not webapp's. Of 48 cron monitors, 5 check in from here and 43 from webapp. apply-sentry-infra.yml also auto-applies the sentry root on push to main via the jianyuan/sentry provider (ADR-031 — no dashboard step; its `paths:` filter is the 3 rule files, not the whole root)
hb3093.technology: HTTPS (cron check-in API + Terraform provider, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN)

w0mx8r.title: Pages the operator — the LARGER of two independent exits (betterstack is the other; a Sentry outage is survivable BY DESIGN). Issue alerts route actions_v2 notify_email → target_type IssueOwners with fallthrough_type ActiveMembers — 21 of the 22 rules; `byok_cap_exceeded` deliberately sets fallthrough_type NoOne. NB IssueOwners has no ownership rule on this project, so the fallthrough is what actually reaches a human: a NoOne rule fires and pages nobody, by design there and a defect anywhere else. The cron monitors open an issue on a missed/failed check-in (failure_issue_threshold = 1) and the uptime monitors on downtime, both of which then fire that same email route

1rbbe5v.title: Pages the operator INDEPENDENTLY of Sentry — the second source. The apex uptime monitor and the inngest/git-data heartbeats alert on downtime/missed beats; on the free tier this is email to the managed recipient, escalating via betteruptime_policy when var.betterstack_paid_tier is set. This is the exit that survives a Sentry-side outage, which is the entire reason the vendor is duplicated
1rbbe5v.technology: Better Stack monitor/heartbeat → email (+ policy escalation on paid tier)

ge4yln.title: Mints packages:read installation token every 20 min (ADR-088)

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest/git-data heartbeats, and a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK self-reports from the deny-all registry host and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is issue-alerts.tf/cron-monitors.tf/uptime-monitors.tf, so versions/main/variables changes do NOT auto-apply). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

Keyless container-image signing/verification (cosign/Fulcio/Rekor). The release CI keyless-signs the pushed web app image digest (this IS a live sigstore call — github → sigstore below). The host then cosign-verifies the signature FULLY OFFLINE against a pinned trusted_root.json — it makes NO live sigstore call at verify time (ADR-087, #6005) — supply-chain authenticity for the shared host (#5933 Item 4).

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.) and MULTIPLE connector replicas: every web host runs cloudflared against it, so CF load-balances across them and `localhost:` means 'whichever replica answered', not 'this host'. Correctness therefore requires every connector host to be a 10.0.1.0/24 member — see ADR-114 (I1/I2) for the invariants and #6416 for the failure. Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.) and MULTIPLE connector replicas: every web host runs cloudflared against it, so CF load-balances across them and `localhost:` means 'whichever replica answered', not 'this host'. Correctness therefore requires every connector host to be a 10.0.1.0/24 member — see ADR-114 (I1/I2) for the invariants and #6416 for the failure. Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

nodes.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel, N connector replicas — 2 today: web-1 + web-2)

description.txt: Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders. Extracted to its own single-host private-net node so exactly-one-scheduler is enforced by topology, unblocking active-active web (ADR-030, ADR-100, #6178)

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is issue-alerts.tf/cron-monitors.tf/uptime-monitors.tf, so versions/main/variables changes do NOT auto-apply). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.) and MULTIPLE connector replicas: every web host runs cloudflared against it, so CF load-balances across them and `localhost:` means 'whichever replica answered', not 'this host'. Correctness therefore requires every connector host to be a 10.0.1.0/24 member — see ADR-114 (I1/I2) for the invariants and #6416 for the failure. Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).
